coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy Z2 only—8–10 miles at conversational pace (140–150 bpm max), rolling terrain if available. Your resting HR is 79 (up from your 30-day average of 62.4) and recovery score sits at 53/100, which tells me you're still in a residual fatigue state from the peak block. You have 57 days to race and are in taper; this is a shakeout day, not a stimulus day. Keep it relaxed, stay in the zone, and let your CNS settle. No tempo, no vert work today.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Ingest 30–40g carbs + electrolytes during the run if it exceeds 75 minutes; keep it simple (sports drink or gels). Post-run, prioritize a meal with 20–30g protein and 80–100g carbs within 90 minutes to shift gears toward recovery and glycogen repletion. Your sleep efficiency last night was solid at 80.2%, but your resting HR elevation suggests glycogen debt or insufficient carb intake over the last 48–72 hours. Bump carbs deliberately today—you're not calorie-restricted in taper.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Target 9+ hours sleep tonight; you got 9.34h two nights ago, which is exactly the threshold you need in taper. The elevated resting HR is a red flag that sleep or fueling has slipped—nail both tonight. Consider a 10–15 minute easy walk or mobility session this evening (not a hard stretch), and keep caffeine intake cut off by 2 PM. If you travel for work today, prioritize sleep hygiene over schedule flexibility.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 8 weeks out from a 2,500m vert alpine 50K on terrain you likely haven't raced before. Taper anxiety is real, but it's also a feature: your body is supercompensating. Trust the 22-week block you've completed. Use this week and next to visualize specific sections of the Julian Alps course—aid station placement, descent strategy, early-race pacing anchors. The real work is done. Your job now is to arrive rested, fueled, and sharp.
